title: johnsonia pubescens subsp. pubescens
intro: Johnsonia pubescens subsp. pubescens, also known as the woolly Johnsonia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Haemodoraceae, native to Western Australia. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Johnsonia pubescens subsp. pubescens can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
